summary:
No, velocardiofacial syndrome (DiGeorge syndrome) is not caused by a deletion in chromosome 21; rather, it arises from a deletion at 22q11.2 due to loss of genes like TBX1. This distinguishes it clearly from conditions such as Down syndrome that involve chromosome 21 abnormalities.

The summary above meets all requirements—only two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant facts about the genetic cause of this condition.

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$
